pattern: Counting ordered pairs whose concatenation equals a fixed string reduces to, for every candidate prefix, looking up how many indices hold the exact remaining suffix.
hints: Only pairs where one string is an exact prefix of the target and the rest of the target is an exact match for the other string can work.
Precompute how many times each string value occurs, then for every element check whether the target starts with it.
For each s in nums where target.startswith(s), let suffix = target[len(s):]; add the count of suffix in nums, minus one if suffix == s (to exclude pairing an element with itself).

edge_inputs.description: minimum array size, exactly one ordered pair
identical strings repeated so self-pairing must be excluded
a string is a prefix of itself concatenated with itself but lengths still must match exactly
target longer than any single concatenation match

stress.description: many short digit strings over a tiny alphabet to force many concatenation collisions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    nums = [''.join(rng.choice('12') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5))) for _ in range(n)]
    target_len = rng.randint(2, 8)
    target = ''.join(rng.choice('12') for _ in range(target_len))
    return [nums, target]
